feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business Technology Platform
feed.subtitle: SAP Business Technology Platform Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Tutorial on BTP Trial
entry.content:
I've come here from here: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-portal-cloud-foundry-getting-started.html

Hello Lindsay and the team,

I have reached Step 2. Point 6. (You’ll receive an email to activate your account. Once you’ve activated your account and press Continue, the Cloud Identity Services Administration Console opens.) 

and been waiting for the handoff email for the last 3 days or so. Should the tutorial be updated with a fork step to continue if the email has not been received? 

I would really like to complete the tutorial, if it is still available to the development community.

Thank you and best regards,

greg

P.S. I have left "Email me when someone replies" checked but never received emails for my prior posts, but that's a different team altogether, methinks.

entry.title: Can an SAP Joule Agent be invoked programmatically?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We are wondering whether it is possible to invoke an SAP Joule Agent programmatically.

Our use case is fairly straightforward: we have a CAP application with a UI where users can provide input. We would like to pass that input to a Joule Agent and have the agent perform actions in other systems or processes. To achieve this, we would need a way to access or invoke the agent programmatically from Node.js, Python etc.

Is there currently a way to invoke Joule Agents programmatically? If not, are there any plans on SAP's roadmap to provide APIs or other integration mechanisms for communicating with Joule Agents?

Best,

entry.title: BTP Trial Registration - Phone Verification Blocked
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

I am trying to register for an SAP BTP Trial account so I can begin my hands-on learning, but I am completely stuck at the telephone number verification step. Every time I input my information and request the verification code, I receive this exact error message:

"We did not send you a code because you are not allowed to request phone calls. Please contact your system administrator."

Because this is a personal trial account for learning purposes, I do not have a system administrator to contact.

My Location Details:

  • Country: Philippines

  • Country Code: +63

(Note: The email address associated with my stuck registration is the same one I am using to post this community question. I can provide my full phone number via Direct Message to an SAP Moderator if needed.)

Troubleshooting Already Attempted:

  • Switched to a mobile hotspot network to change my IP/routing.

  • Cleared browser cache, cookies, and tried Incognito/Private browsing.

  • Verified that my phone number formatting is correct and completed the CAPTCHA successfully.

Could an SAP moderator please assist me with overriding or whitelisting this registration from the backend so I can access the BTP Cockpit?

Thank you!

entry.title: How to send data extracts and replications from SAP S4 to MS Fabrics
entry.content:
Hello Mates,

Good day!

How to integrate and send data extracts and replications from SAP S4 HANA Private Cloud(RISE) to MS Fabrics via Standalone SAP SLT DMIS2020? Is this considered an strategic solution to use this option quickly?

Only 2 option comes in to my mind as optimal one is SAP datasphere and another is Standalone SAP SLT.

Please require your kind inputs. Appreciate it!

 

Thanks and Regards,

Rajesh PS

entry.title: Production Logging Strategy for Client-Side UI5 Application Deployed on SAP BTP
entry.content:
We are building an app using Ui5 freestyle that acts as a side-by-side extension in Sales and Service Cloud Version 2 (SSCv2).

Our goal is to add logging features to the app to assist developers in debugging and troubleshooting issues in the production environment.

Since we are not creating any backend services, we cannot utilize the Cloud logging services in BTP. This app is purely a client-side Ui5 application.

Could you please advise on how to implement logging within our app so that developers can access the logs when the app is deployed on BTP and running in SSCv2 as a mashup

entry.title: Do ERP Systems Really Need Machine Learning? Exploring SAP HANA PAL in SAP BTP
entry.content:
Introduction

Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems have traditionally been designed to manage and process business transactions efficiently. Their primary role has been to capture, store, and organize business data across functions such as Sales, Procurement, Finance, Manufacturing, and Supply Chain.

However, modern organizations expect more from their ERP systems. Businesses are no longer satisfied with simply storing historical data; they want systems that can analyze information, identify patterns, predict future outcomes, and support decision-making.

This raises an important question:

Do ERP systems really need Machine Learning?

The answer depends on the business problem being addressed. Machine Learning should not be implemented merely because the technology is available. Instead, it should be used where it can provide measurable business value.

As part of my research on SAP HANA Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) Procedures in SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), I explored how built-in Machine Learning algorithms can be applied directly within the SAP ecosystem to solve real business challenges.

This blog summarizes some of the key Machine Learning capabilities available through SAP HANA PAL and their relevance in ERP scenarios.

What is SAP HANA PAL?

The Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) is a collection of Machine Learning and statistical algorithms that run directly inside the SAP HANA database.

Rather than exporting data to external Machine Learning platforms, PAL enables organizations to perform predictive analytics where the data already resides.

Some key advantages include:

  • Reduced data movement

  • Improved performance

  • Better data governance

  • Integration with SAP applications and business processes

  • Real-time analytics capabilities

PAL provides algorithms for:

  • Time Series Forecasting

  • Classification

  • Regression

  • Clustering

  • Association Rules

  • Outlier Detection

  • Recommendation Scenarios

Let's explore how these capabilities can be used in ERP environments.

1. Time Series Forecasting

Forecasting is one of the most valuable Machine Learning applications in ERP systems.

Organizations continuously need to estimate future demand, sales, inventory levels, and procurement requirements.

Using historical business data, forecasting algorithms can predict future trends and support proactive planning.

Typical ERP Use Cases

Sales Forecasting

Predict future sales based on historical transactions and seasonal patterns.

Production Planning

Estimate manufacturing demand and optimize production schedules.

Inventory Planning

Maintain optimal stock levels while reducing overstocking and stock shortages.

Procurement Planning

Predict material requirements and improve purchasing decisions.

Business Value

  • Improved demand planning

  • Reduced inventory carrying costs

  • Better resource utilization

  • Enhanced supply chain efficiency

2. Classification

Classification algorithms predict predefined categories or outcomes based on historical data.

In ERP systems, classification can help organizations estimate probabilities and support decision-making processes.

Example: Quotation Conversion Prediction

Many companies generate thousands of sales quotations every year. However, only a portion of these quotations eventually become sales orders.

Using classification models, organizations can analyze historical quotation data and predict:

  • Which quotations are likely to convert

  • Which opportunities require additional attention

  • Which customers have higher purchase probability

Additional ERP Use Cases

Customer Churn Prediction

Identify customers who are likely to stop doing business with the company.

Supplier Risk Classification

Classify suppliers based on delivery performance, quality metrics, and compliance factors.

Payment Default Prediction

Identify customers with a higher likelihood of delayed or missed payments.

Business Value

  • Improved sales effectiveness

  • Better risk management

  • Enhanced customer retention

  • Stronger supplier governance

3. Regression

While classification predicts categories, regression predicts continuous numerical values.

Regression algorithms are highly useful when organizations need estimates for future business metrics.

ERP Use Cases

Revenue Prediction

Forecast future revenue based on historical trends and business drivers.

Sales Value Forecasting

Estimate the expected value of upcoming sales opportunities.

Procurement Cost Estimation

Predict material procurement costs using historical pricing data.

Product Price Forecasting

Estimate future product pricing trends.

Customer Lifetime Value Analysis

Predict the long-term value of customer relationships.

Business Value

  • Improved budgeting and planning

  • Better financial forecasting

  • Data-driven pricing strategies

  • Enhanced profitability analysis

4. Association Rules

Association Rule Mining identifies relationships between products, services, and customer transactions.

This technique is widely used in retail, e-commerce, and sales analytics.

Example

Suppose customers frequently purchase:

  • Product A

  • Product B

together within the same transaction.

Association Rule algorithms can discover this pattern and provide actionable recommendations.

ERP Use Cases

Product Recommendations

Suggest related products during sales processes.

Cross-Selling Opportunities

Identify complementary products that increase sales revenue.

Product Bundling

Create optimized product packages based on purchasing behavior.

Customer Buying Pattern Analysis

Understand customer purchasing habits and preferences.

Business Value

  • Increased sales revenue

  • Improved customer experience

  • Better marketing effectiveness

  • Stronger product strategy

5. Clustering

Clustering is an unsupervised Machine Learning technique used to group similar records without predefined labels.

It helps organizations uncover hidden patterns within business data.

ERP Use Cases

Customer Segmentation

Group customers based on purchasing behavior, revenue, geography, or demographics.

Supplier Segmentation

Categorize suppliers according to performance, risk, or strategic importance.

Product Segmentation

Identify product groups with similar sales or profitability characteristics.

Regional Demand Analysis

Analyze demand patterns across different regions.

Business Value

  • Better customer targeting

  • Improved supplier management

  • More effective marketing campaigns

  • Enhanced strategic planning

Do ERP Systems Really Need Machine Learning?

The answer is:

Not every ERP process requires Machine Learning.

Core ERP functionality such as transaction processing, accounting, order management, and inventory tracking can continue operating effectively without predictive models.

However, when organizations seek answers to questions such as:

  • What will happen next?

  • Which customers are likely to buy?

  • Which suppliers pose risks?

  • How much inventory will be needed?

  • What revenue can be expected?

Machine Learning becomes highly valuable.

ERP systems contain vast amounts of historical business data. Machine Learning enables organizations to transform that data into actionable insights and intelligent decision support.

Key Takeaway

Machine Learning should not be implemented simply because it is available.

The real objective should always be:

"Which business problem are we trying to solve?"

When applied to the right use cases, Machine Learning can transform ERP systems from transaction-processing platforms into intelligent business platforms.

One of the most exciting aspects of SAP HANA PAL is that these capabilities can be executed directly within the SAP ecosystem, reducing the need for external Machine Learning platforms while keeping analytics close to the data.

As I continue exploring SAP HANA PAL Procedures in SAP BTP, it is becoming increasingly clear that Forecasting, Classification, Regression, Association Rules, and Clustering have significant potential to bring intelligence into ERP processes and help organizations make smarter decisions.

References

  • SAP HANA Predictive Analysis Library (PAL)

  • SAP HANA Machine Learning Capabilities

  • SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP)

  • SAP HANA Cloud Predictive Analytics

entry.title: Content Agent file upload Public API Returning 404 Not Found.
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We are encountering an issue while calling the Content Agent File Upload API.

API Endpoint (taken from the Content Agent documentation):
https://content-agent-engine.cfapps.ap21.hana.ondemand.com/files/upload

Response:

{ "type": "about:blank", "title": "Not Found", "status": 404, "detail": "No static resource files/upload.", "instance": "/files/upload" }

We are using credentials from the Content Agent Service Application Plan to generate the access token and invoke the API.

The endpoint above was taken from the Content Agent API documentation. However, we are receiving a 404 Not Found response when attempting to access it.

Could you please investigate this issue and confirm whether the endpoint is correct and currently supported? If there is an updated endpoint, additional configuration required, or any prerequisite steps that we may have missed, please let us know.

entry.title: Are SAP BTP, SAP Build, Generative AI Hub and SAP HANA Cloud restricted to RISE with SAP customers,
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'd like to clarify the licensing eligibility for several SAP cloud products and would appreciate input from the community (and ideally from SAP colleagues with a licensing background).

Context: We are advising a customer who currently runs SAP S/4HANA on-premise (no RISE with SAP contract, no S/4HANA Cloud Private/Public Edition). The customer is interested in adopting the following services:

  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)
  • SAP Build (Apps / Process Automation / Work Zone)
  • SAP Generative AI Hub (within SAP AI Core)
  • SAP HANA Cloud

My questions:

  1. Are any of these products contractually restricted to RISE with SAP customers, or can a pure on-premise customer subscribe to them directly (e.g., via CPEA, Pay-As-You-Go, or Subscription model)?
  2. Which capabilities are only included as part of the RISE with SAP Premium Plus package, and which can be purchased standalone regardless of the ERP deployment model?
  3. Are there any technical prerequisites or limitations (e.g., required S/4HANA Cloud edition, specific connectivity setup) when consuming Generative AI Hub or HANA Cloud from an on-premise S/4HANA landscape?
  4. Does the answer differ between commercial entitlement (what you can buy) and functional integration scope (what you can actually connect to your on-premise system)?

Any pointers to official SAP documentation, product availability matrices, or licensing guides would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: @sap/cds-rfc: RFC_COMMUNICATION_FAILURE (NIECONN_BROKEN) in BAS using cds bind --exec, but works aft
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am trying to run a CAP Node.js application locally in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) using hybrid mode (cds bind --exec). The application connects to an on-premise SAP system via Destination Service + Connectivity Service + Cloud Connector using @SAP/cds-rfc.

The exact same application works perfectly when deployed to SAP BTP Cloud Foundry, but fails when run locally in BAS.

Environment

  • SAP Business Application Studio

  • Node.js: 22.13.1

  • @SAP/cds: 9.9.1

  • @SAP/cds-dk: 9.9.1

  • @SAP/cds-rfc: 2.2.1

  • @SAP-rfc/node-rfc-library: 1.1.2

  • @SAP-cloud-sdk/connectivity: 4.7.0

Hybrid setup

Service bindings are created successfully:

cds bind --to navisaccess-auth
cds bind --to navisaccess-connectivity
cds bind --to navisaccess-destination

Running:

cds bind --exec -- node rfctest.js

correctly injects all Cloud Foundry service bindings.

Minimal reproducible example

const cds = require("@sap/cds");

(async () => {
    try {
        console.log("Connecting...");

        const client = await cds.connect.to("P23_ABAP", {
            credentials: {
                destination: "P23_ABAP"
            }
        });

        console.log("Connected.");

        console.log("Calling RFC_PING...");

        const result = await client.send("RFC_PING", {});

        console.log(result);
    } catch (err) {
        console.error(err);
    }
})();

Output

Connecting...
Connected.
Calling RFC_PING...

RFCException NIECONN_BROKEN :
unable to receive data from connectivityproxy.internal.cf.ap10.hana.ondemand.com:20001

RFC_COMMUNICATION_FAILURE

Stack trace points to:

RFCService.begin()
createBapiClientConnectionHandler()
RFCClientAddonConnectionHandler.open()

What I have verified

  • cds bind works successfully.

  • Destination Service binding is successful.

  • Connectivity Service binding is successful.

  • XSUAA binding is successful.

  • cds.connect.to() succeeds.

  • The destination is resolved successfully.

  • The RFC client is created successfully.

  • The failure occurs only when the first RFC call (RFC_PING or GET_SYSTEM_TIME_REMOTE) is executed.

  • The exact same code works after deploying the application to Cloud Foundry using cf push.

  • It has has never been tested locally in BAS.

My question

Is RFC access through @SAP/cds-rfc + SAP Connectivity Service + Cloud Connector officially supported when running locally using cds bind --exec (hybrid mode)?

If yes, is there any additional configuration required for BAS or hybrid execution?

Or is RFC communication through Cloud Connector only supported for applications running inside the Cloud Foundry runtime?

Any guidance or documentation would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Unexpected Connections Consumption in SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently implementing a Supplier Portal using SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition.

Our current subscription includes:

1,000 Active Users
0 Connections

Authentication is performed through SAP Cloud Identity Services (Identity Authentication - IAS) as our Identity Provider (IdP).

Due to an omission during the commercial contracting process, our subscription was provisioned with Active Users only, while the Connections metric was not included. We are already discussing this situation with our SAP Account Team, but we would like to better understand both the technical behavior and whether anyone has experienced a similar situation.

During our testing, we observed that every external login generates Connections, even though our subscription does not include this metric.

From a technical perspective, we would like to know:

1. Is the generation of the Connections metric mandatory in SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition?
2. Is there any way to completely prevent the generation of the Connections metric through Role Collections, authorizations or SAP Build Work Zone configuration?

From a commercial perspective, we would also appreciate guidance from anyone who has experienced a similar situation.

Any guidance or shared experience would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: BTP Free Teir or Pay as you Go for Small startups
entry.content:
Hi, 

we are a small startup planning to develop some agentic solutions for which we need access to Joule Studio. Does BTP trail account provide access to Joule Studio ?  is Pay-As-You-Go account is the way to go for all solutions access ?  Does SAP Provide BTP PYAG account for small startups ?  When i tried to sign-up for PAYG account, it took me to trial. Not sure what is happening.   Did anybody experience this? What is the best way to go about this?

 

Regards

pallayya

entry.title: BTP Trail account: Cloud identity services Access denier error
entry.content:
Hi Team,

While accessing the Cloud identity services from BTP Trail account getting 

"You entered a valid URL, but you are not authorized to view the content; contact your system administrator"

Getting this error for quite some time, was there any issue in CIS?

Kindly assist..

Thanks in advance.

Regards,

Venkat

entry.title: SAP BTP Trial phone verification blocked - not allowed to request phone calls
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am trying to activate my SAP BTP Trial account, but I cannot complete the telephone verification step.

After entering my phone number, completing the CAPTCHA, and clicking “Request Code”, I receive this error message:

"We did not send you a code because you are not allowed to request phone calls. Please contact your system administrator."

This is a personal/student SAP BTP Trial account, not a company-managed account.

I already contacted SAPBTPTrialSupport@sap.com and sso@sap.com, but I received an autoreply asking me to check SAP Community.

Could someone from SAP please advise how I can unblock the phone verification or manually verify my account?

Country: Tunisia
SAP User ID: P2012826210

Thank you.
Best regards,
Adam El Hajri

entry.title: BTP Solution Architect Certification guidance
entry.content:
Dear BTP Experts-

I have been looking into the SAP BTP Solution Architect certification path. While researching, I found that there are many blogs, YouTube channels, and learning resources available, but they are scattered across different places. I was wondering if there is a structured learning path or course that follows the right sequence and is effective for certification preparation.

If you have already completed the SAP Certification– SAP BTP  Solution Architect certification, or if you are currently preparing for it, could you please recommend the best approach or learning path? I would really appreciate your guidance on what worked well for you.   SAP Learning Platform has courses in free. Were that sufficient or I need to focus on any other.

If there are any additional resources, structured courses,  mock exams, or YouTube channels that you found particularly helpful, I'd be grateful if you could share them.

Thank you in advance!

Best Regards,

Pramod Upadhyay

entry.title: Practice System for SAP BTP Admin Certification
entry.content:
Hi There.

I am preparing for the certification on SAP BTP Administration. The name of the Learning Journey in SAP Learning Hub is "Administrating SAP Business Technology Platform". 

Please let me know if the certification exam is based on tasks to be done in the BTP instance.

Is there a practice system available for this learning journey? I do not see one listed in SAP Learning Hub for this practice, please advise what is recommended to prepare for the certification exam.

Having a practice system is essential for this kind of hands-on learning journeys, as that will greatly improve the learner's chances of remembering the concepts and preparing for the exam.

entry.title: SAP Document AI Basic UI Subscription Fails in BTP Trial Despite Active IAS OIDC Trust
entry.content:
# SAP Document AI Basic UI Subscription Fails in BTP Trial Despite Active IAS OIDC Trust

Hello SAP Community,

I am trying to set up **SAP Document AI in an SAP BTP Trial account**, but the subscription to the SAP Document AI Basic UI continues to fail.

## Environment

- SAP BTP Trial
- Global account: `e14e****trial`
- Subaccount: `trial_Document_AI_****`
- Region: **US East (VA), AWS**
- Cloud Foundry environment: Enabled
- SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant: **Trial (USA)**
- IAS tenant domain: `ahw5****.trial-accounts.ondemand.com`

## Current Setup

The following components have been configured successfully.

### 1. SAP Document AI Trial Service Instance

- Service: `SAP Document AI Trial`
- Plan: `blocks_of_100`
- Status: `Created`
- Service key: Created successfully

### 2. SAP Cloud Identity Services

- Plan: `default`
- Subscription status: `Subscribed`
- Cloud Identity Services tenant: Activated

### 3. OIDC Trust

The OIDC trust between the SAP BTP subaccount and SAP Cloud Identity Services has been established.

- Custom Identity Provider status: `Active`
- Origin key: `sap.custom`
- Available for user logon: `Yes`
- Protocol: `OpenID Connect`

The Cloud Identity Services tenant settings also show that the BTP global account and the Document AI subaccount are connected.

- Global account identifier: `8b59****153c`
- Subaccount identifier: `95a3****85fc`

### 4. Entitlements

The following entitlements are assigned to the same subaccount.

- `SAP Document AI Trial`
- Plan: `blocks_of_100`
- Subaccount assignment: `1 unit`
- `SAP Document AI`
- Plan: `default (Application)`

The remaining global quota for `blocks_of_100` is `0 units` because the available unit is already assigned to this subaccount.

### 5. Role Collection

The SAP Document AI role collection was also created successfully.

## Problem

When I subscribe to the following application:

- Application: `SAP Document AI`
- Plan: `default`
- Application technical name: `document-information-extraction-trial-application-ias`
- Subscription ID: `28cd****daf1`

the subscription ends with the following status:

> **Subscription Failed**

The detailed message only states:

> Couldn't subscribe to SAP Document AI. Try again. If the problem persists, please post a question to SAP Community.

The **Go to Application** button remains disabled.

## Additional Observations

The Service Marketplace contains two separate entries:

- `SAP Document AI`
- `SAP Document AI Trial`

The `SAP Document AI Trial` service instance using the `blocks_of_100` plan was created successfully.

Only the `SAP Document AI` Basic UI subscription using the `default` plan fails.

In the SAP Cloud Identity Services administration console, only the following system applications are visible:

- Administration Console
- User Profile

No SAP Document AI application appears to have been provisioned in the Cloud Identity Services tenant.

I also deleted the failed SAP Document AI UI subscription and tried subscribing again after the OIDC trust was fully established, but the same error occurred.

## Questions

1. Is this a known provisioning issue with the new **SAP Document AI Trial** setup?
2. Are there any additional prerequisites for subscribing to `document-information-extraction-trial-application-ias`?
3. Is there an audit log or detailed provisioning log where the actual subscription failure reason can be checked?
4. Could the separate `SAP Document AI` and `SAP Document AI Trial` Marketplace entries affect the Trial Basic UI provisioning?
5. Is any additional configuration required in SAP Cloud Identity Services before subscribing to the Basic UI?

Any guidance would be appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Document AI – Integration from Multiple External Web Systems (PDF Upload) & Response Data / Rete
entry.content:
Hi all,

We are currently designing an integration architecture involving SAP Document AI and would appreciate some clarification from the community or SAP experts.

We operate multiple external web systems, and users upload PDF documents through each of these systems. We are evaluating whether these PDFs can be sent to SAP Document AI for processing (OCR / data extraction), as part of a broader automation/AI transformation initiative we're currently designing.

### Questions

**1. Feasibility**

Is it possible for PDF documents uploaded from multiple external web systems to be sent to SAP Document AI via API for processing (OCR/data extraction)?

**2. Response data and reusability**

When calling the API, what data is returned in the response (e.g., extracted fields, confidence scores, whether the original document is included, etc.)?

- Is this response meant to be consumed immediately by the calling system only (synchronous, one-time use)?
- Or is the extraction result persisted on the SAP Document AI side, so that it can be retrieved later by a different system or at a different point in time (i.e., queried again after the fact)?

**3. Document retention**

Is the original PDF file itself stored on the SAP side after being submitted? If so, how long is it retained (e.g., indefinitely, or deleted after a certain period)?

---

Any insight — whether from documentation, hands-on experience, or official SAP guidance — would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP Build Code BAS -/ui5-create-app fails with "@ui5/mcp-server" error and Accept Application button
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to create a freestyle SAPUI5 application in SAP Build Code (BAS) using the new Joule command:
/ui5-create-app

Prompt used

/ui5-create-app Create a freestyle SAPUI5 application for the Manage Sales Order CAP service.
Use the OData V4 service:
/service/manage_Sales_Order_App

CAP Project Information
I already have a CAP project generated successfully using Joule.
Project structure:
db/
srv/
app/
The OData service is available and generated correctly.

Joule Response
Joule returns the following proposal:
Let's configure your Manage Sales Order app using JavaScript instead of TypeScript.
Namespace: com.myorg.managesalesorder
Base Path:
/home/user/projects/Manage_Sales_Order_App/app
Create App Directory: true
OData V4 URL:
service/manage_Sales_Order_App/
OData Entity Set:
SalesOrders
TypeScript: false
Once you're ready, you can accept the application proposal
by pressing the "Accept Application" button.
Issue
The "Accept Application" button never appears.

At the same time, I can see the following error in the BAS Error panel:
Failed to generate app proposal:

Cannot find package '@ui5/mcp-server'
imported from

/extbin/local/openvscode-server/extensions/
sap-ux-help-extension-1.28.0/dist/extension-min.cjs

Did you mean to import
"@ui5/mcp-server/lib/api.js"?

Source:
SAP Fiori Tools - Guided Development

Troubleshooting Performed
I have already tried:

Refreshing BAS browser window
Opening a new Joule conversation
Using a simplified /ui5-create-app prompt
Creating a new Development Space
Reopening the project

The same error occurs every time.

Environment
SAP Build Code / SAP Business Application Studio
SAP Fiori Tools - Guided Development
Extension version reported in error:
sap-ux-help-extension-1.28.0
Questions
Is this a known issue with the SAP Fiori Tools / UI5 Create Application assistant?
Has anyone encountered the missing package error:
@ui5/mcp-server
Is there any workaround available until the issue is fixed?
Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: SAP BTP: Error activating BAS extensions
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

opening my dev space in BAS I get the following error and some core extensions, such as the SAP HANA Database Explorer, are no longer working

I tried recreating the dev space without success. The error persists in the new dev space, and I cannot even clone projects from Git.

I have attached the error log for reference

registry=https://registry.npmjs.org
@bas-dev:registry=https://common.repositories.cloud.sap/artifactory/api/npm/devx-npm-live/
//common.repositories.cloud.sap/artifactory/api/npm/devx-npm-live/:_authToken=<REDACTED>
//common.repositories.cloud.sap/artifactory/api/npm/devx-npm-live/:always-auth=true
@sapdmz:registry=https://common.repositories.cloud.sap/artifactory/api/npm/build.releases.npm/
//common.repositories.cloud.sap/artifactory/api/npm/build.releases.npm/:_authToken=<REDACTED>
//common.repositories.cloud.sap/artifactory/api/npm/build.releases.npm/:always-auth=true
@sap-buildcode:registry=https://common.repositories.cloud.sap/artifactory/api/npm/deploy.releases.npm/
//common.repositories.cloud.sap/artifactory/api/npm/deploy.releases.npm/:_authToken=<REDACTED>
//common.repositories.cloud.sap/artifactory/api/npm/deploy.releases.npm/:always-auth=true
@devx-wing:registry=https://common.repositories.cloud.sap/artifactory/api/npm/devx-npm-live/
@sap-cds:registry=https://common.repositories.cloud.sap/artifactory/api/npm/deploy.releases.npm/
@sap-lcnc:registry=https://common.repositories.cloud.sap/artifactory/api/npm/deploy.releases.npm/
@ext-dptvsc-npm-vscode:registry=https://common.repositories.cloud.sap/artifactory/api/npm/deploy.releases.npm/
@ext-dptvsc-npm-dev:registry=https://common.repositories.cloud.sap/artifactory/api/npm/deploy.releases.npm/
@sapext:registry=https://common.repositories.cloud.sap/artifactory/api/npm/deploy.releases.npm/

registry=https://registry.npmjs.org

Can anyone help me figure this out?

Thanks

entry.title: X-CSRF token missing error in SAP BPA
entry.content:
We have getting the below error in SAP BPA. 

"Invoke action Decision Final Approval DAG" failed Error message: Action '9d154664-35d3-4775-xxxx-ea6ee33ac599' failed with the following error: 'Error thrown from action runtime library: Could not fetch X-CSRF token for action 'Invoke action Decision.': X-CSRF token missing in the response headers of GET call to /'. Log ID: 63ad02b2-b733-4aef-xxxx-cb1b078-bs10 Destination: s4hana-http-principalpropagation Action Identifier: post_Decision Remaining Retries: 0  

Has anyone faced this issue, the destination connection test in BTP is working fine. Also cloud connector connectivity is also working fine. Can anyone help me!
